stem: On the q-sphere of this state, the node for |01> is drawn in a different color from the node for |10>, and the two nodes are the same size. What does that color difference encode?
code:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector
from qiskit.visualization import plot_state_qsphere

qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
qc.x(0)
qc.z(0)
state = Statevector.from_instruction(qc)
fig = plot_state_qsphere(state)

options.text: The amplitude magnitude — the |01> node stands for a larger amplitude than |10>.
The relative phase of the amplitude — the |01> branch carries phase π while |10> has phase 0.
The Hamming weight (number of 1-bits) of the basis state.
Nothing meaningful — q-sphere nodes are all the same color and any difference is a rendering artifact.

explanation.correct: On a q-sphere a node's SIZE encodes the amplitude magnitude and its COLOR encodes the amplitude's complex phase. Here |01> and |10> have equal magnitude (same size) but the Z gate multiplies the |01> branch by −1, giving it phase π versus phase 0 for |10> — so the colors differ.

distractors.A: The two amplitudes have equal magnitude (the nodes are the same size); magnitude is shown by size, not color.
distractors.C: |01> and |10> have the same Hamming weight (one 1-bit each), so a color difference cannot be encoding Hamming weight — it is the phase that differs.
distractors.D: q-sphere nodes ARE color-coded by phase; the difference is meaningful, not an artifact.

verification.proof_script:
import numpy as np
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.quantum_info import Statevector

qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
qc.x(0)
qc.z(0)
sv = Statevector.from_instruction(qc)
ampl = {str(k): v for k, v in sv.to_dict().items() if abs(v) > 1e-9}

a01 = ampl.get("01", 0)
a10 = ampl.get("10", 0)
m01 = round(abs(a01), 4)
m10 = round(abs(a10), 4)
p01 = round(float(np.angle(a01)), 4)
p10 = round(float(np.angle(a10)), 4)
hw01 = "01".count("1")
hw10 = "10".count("1")

mag_equal = abs(m01 - m10) < 1e-6
phase_diff_pi = abs(abs(p01 - p10) - np.pi) < 1e-3

evidence = {}
proven = []
mA = m01 > m10
evidence["A"] = f"|amp(01)|={m01}, |amp(10)|={m10}; magnitudes equal={mag_equal}; '01 larger magnitude' match={mA}"
if mA:
    proven.append("A")
mB = mag_equal and phase_diff_pi
evidence["B"] = f"phase(01)={p01} rad vs phase(10)={p10} rad (differ by pi={phase_diff_pi}); magnitudes equal={mag_equal} -> color encodes relative phase, match={mB}"
if mB:
    proven.append("B")
mC = hw01 != hw10
evidence["C"] = f"Hamming weights 01->{hw01}, 10->{hw10} (equal); a color difference cannot encode Hamming weight, match={mC}"
if mC:
    proven.append("C")
mD = (not phase_diff_pi) and mag_equal and (p01 == p10)
evidence["D"] = f"amplitudes differ in phase ({p01} vs {p10}); nodes are not identical, so not an artifact, match={mD}"
if mD:
    proven.append("D")

emit_verdict({
    "answer": proven,
    "evidence": evidence,
    "observed": {"amp_01": str(np.round(a01, 4)), "amp_10": str(np.round(a10, 4))},
})
